rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as a follow-up to Novemsky and Kahneman (2005), stating that the earlier article proposed intention-based boundaries of loss aversion and that this commentary expands that idea. The cited paper is not a background list item; it is the core prior claim that motivates the entire rejoinder. The citing paper then develops additional explanatory mechanisms (e.g., emotional attachment, cognitive focus, reference-point expectations) for the intention effects introduced in the cited work.
relation_description: The citing article directly builds on the cited article's intention-based boundary account of loss aversion and extends it by developing mechanism-level explanations for why intentions moderate loss aversion. It treats the cited paper as the immediate platform for further conceptual elaboration.
